title: The Struggle Is My Life
description: The Struggle Is My Life is a non-fiction by Nelson Mandela. Banned in Apartheid South Africa until 1990.
descriptionParagraphs: The Struggle Is My Life is a non-fiction by Nelson Mandela. Banned in Apartheid South Africa until 1990.

Its significance comes from how questions of race, empire, or hierarchy remain live enough to provoke official suppression. As a non-fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in South Africa. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned in Apartheid South Africa until 1990. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: The Struggle Is My Life entered censorship debates as a non-fiction associated with history, political memory, and race. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity and racial politics.
